status: strict first Laguerre positivity for exact H_t on the complete ray t*log(x/(4*pi))>=25, including all cutoff transitions; not a proof of Lambda<=0 or RH
proof_boundary: This artifact proves L_t(x)>0 for every real x satisfying 0<t<=1/2 and t*log(x/(4*pi))>=25, with no cutoff-distance restriction. It does not cover 0<t*log(x/(4*pi))<25, all real x and t, Lambda<=0, or RH.

exact.boundary_spacing: x_(N+1)-x_N=4*pi*(2N+1); for L>=50 a radius-1/4 disk crosses at most one cutoff boundary
exact.fixed_center: Use S_(N_x,t)(z)=B_t(z)f_(N_x,t)(z) throughout the disk, even where the theorem-prescribed cutoff N_z differs from N_x
exact.theorem: For every real x on the ray, C_t[H_t/A_t](x)>3/40*L^2 and L_t(x)>0

rows.claim: A Cauchy disk on the dominant ray can meet no more than one Riemann-Siegel cutoff transition.
rows.formula: x_(N+1)-x_N=4*pi*(2N+1); for L>=50 a radius-1/4 disk crosses at most one cutoff boundary
rows.proof_boundary: Exact cutoff spacing plus the Arb lower bound on N.

rows.claim: One may keep the center cutoff fixed as an analytic main sum throughout a transition-crossing disk.
rows.formula: Use S_(N_x,t)(z)=B_t(z)f_(N_x,t)(z) throughout the disk, even where the theorem-prescribed cutoff N_z differs from N_x
rows.proof_boundary: The fixed finite sum remains holomorphic; only its comparison with the prescribed sum changes.

rows.claim: The discrepancy between adjacent prescribed cutoffs is one strongly suppressed two-saddle block.
rows.proof_boundary: Uses tL>=25, the published Re(s_*) bound, and the gamma/kappa bounds.

rows.claim: Arb bounds the raw adjacent-cutoff jump far below the available collar slack.
rows.proof_boundary: Outward-rounded evaluation at the monotone endpoint L=50.

rows.claim: The same raw Cauchy collar bound now holds whether or not the disk crosses a cutoff.
rows.proof_boundary: Combines the fixed-cell published remainder with at most one adjacent block.

rows.claim: Strict first Laguerre positivity holds for the exact Newman heat flow on the entire dominant-saddle ray.
rows.formula: For every real x on the ray, C_t[H_t/A_t](x)>3/40*L^2 and L_t(x)>0
rows.proof_boundary: No cutoff-distance exception remains; the tL threshold remains essential.

rows.claim: The discrete cutoff-transition collars are no longer an open high-frequency gap.
The sole remaining global Newman-tail regime is the shrinking near-zero-time arithmetic layer.
